title: alnus viridis subsp. microphylla
intro: Alnus viridis subsp. microphylla, also known as the mountain alder or small-leaf alder, is a subspecies of shrub or small tree that belongs to the alder family, characterized by its small leaves and ability to grow in wet environments. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Alnus viridis subsp. microphylla include the potential to reduce fever and treat various infections, as well as its ability to aid in the healing process of wounds. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
